summary: Scorching China>Rider, epic Playing In The Band, tight Scarlet Begonias
blurb: The June 22 1974 Miami show delivers a blistering second set with a soaring China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and an expansive Playing In The Band. Jerry is on fire, Phil lays down a hypnotic bass groove, and the band rides a tight groove from Scarlet Begonias through the Eyes‑Wharf‑Sugar medley. Recommended for anyone chasing classic ’74 energy.
review: June 22, 1974 at the Jai‑Alai Fronton remains one of the high‑water marks of the Dead’s summer ’74 run. From the opening “The Promised Land” → “Bertha”, the first set builds a steady groove that peaks with a fiery rendition of “Greatest Story Ever Told” and a beautifully twangy “Me And Bobby McGee”. The real fireworks erupt in the second set: a flawless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, followed by an inspired “Eyes Of The World” → Wharf Rat → Sugar Magnolia chain that keeps the dance floor humming. The centerpiece is a monumental 28‑minute “Playing In The Band”, where Jerry’s pure tone and soaring solos carry the band from start to finish, while Phil’s bass line drives the jam with thunderous authority. Keith’s organ swells on Scarlet Begonias and the “Greatest Story” solo, and Bill’s drumming stays solid throughout, even if a few audience tapes mute the low end. Highlights like “Deal”, “Johnny B. Goode” and the encore bring the night home with classic Dead joy. Across all sources the performance is described as “on fire”, “locked in”, and among the best of June ’74 – a must‑listen for any serious Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across seven of nine recordings, with repeated praise for Jerry’s fire, the epic Playing In The Band, and seamless segues (China>Rider, Eyes‑Wharf‑Sugar). No consistent negative comments about performance; only mixed notes on recording quality. This consistency merits a high positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent matrix blend of soundboard and audience, crisp instrument separation, vivid crowd ambience, and no drop‑outs – the clearest window into this legendary performance.

key_highlights: Epic 28‑minute Playing In The Band that lifts the second set.
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition.
Powerful Eyes Of The World → Wharf Rat → Sugar Magnolia medley.
Stellar Scarlet Begonias and The Greatest Story Ever Told.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is repeatedly praised as ‘on fire’, delivering pure tone, inspired solos and carrying the show from start to finish.
band_performance.Phil: Lesch’s bass is described as hypnotic and thunderous, anchoring the long jams while occasionally drawing mixed remarks on a specific solo in Eyes Of The World.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s guitar work faces no major criticism; vocals are noted as low on some audience mixes but otherwise solid.
band_performance.Keith: Keith Godchaux’s organ and piano shine on Scarlet Begonias, the Greatest Story Ever Told and Me And Bobby McGee, though a few recordings note his mix can sit low.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann provides solid, well‑balanced drumming throughout; some audience tapes report low drum levels but the overall groove stays tight.
